title: Nvidia to invest up to $2.1 billion in IREN as part of AI data centre deal
snippet: Nvidia is investing up to $2.1 billion in data centre operator IREN to deploy 5 gigawatts of AI infrastructure, addressing the immense demand for computing power. This partnership aims to accelerate AI factory deployment by integrating Nvidia's architecture with IREN's operations, with future focus

title: Rave files antitrust lawsuit against Apple over removal of video-sharing app
snippet: Rave, a video sharing app, has sued Apple in a US federal court. Rave claims Apple removed its app from the App Store to promote its own SharePlay feature. The company seeks significant damages and reinstatement. Rave has also filed similar lawsuits in other countries.

title: Tech is turning increasingly to religion in a quest to create ethical AI
snippet: Tech companies are seeking guidance from faith leaders to infuse morality into AI development, a shift from Silicon Valley's past skepticism. The inaugural "Faith-AI Covenant" roundtable brought together religious representatives and AI firms to discuss ethical principles.

title: Cloudflare to cut about 20% workforce as AI adoption reshapes operations
snippet: Cloudflare announced significant layoffs, cutting approximately 20% of its workforce, impacting over 1,100 global employees. This restructuring is driven by the company's rapid adoption of artificial intelligence tools and a shift towards an 'agentic AI-first operating model.' Despite stronger first

title: Apple, Meta warn Canadian bill could force them to weaken encryption
snippet: Apple and Meta are opposing Canada's Bill C-22, arguing it could force them to break device and service encryption. Law enforcement claims the bill aids investigations, but tech companies warn it risks weakening user security by potentially requiring backdoors or government spyware.

title: PoS terminal supply squeeze; Skyroot CEO on unicorn flight
